purpose: Evidence gate for external research claims used by SCBE benchmark, agent-safety, and semantic-geometry docs.

status_levels.verified: Supported by a primary source, official source, or paper page with enough detail to cite.
status_levels.watch: Plausible or useful, but only supported by a preprint, vendor blog, or single-source claim.
status_levels.reject_for_public_claims: Do not use as a public fact without stronger evidence.

claims.claim: SWE-Bench Pro contains 1,865 problems sourced from 41 maintained repositories, including public, held-out, and commercial/proprietary sets.
claims.repo_action: Keep SWE-Bench Pro in Aether Coding Score real-repo-repair lane, but require official/full-run or reproducible subset evidence before assigning points.
claims.public_language: SWE-Bench Pro is a valid target lane for long-horizon repository repair. No SCBE score is claimed until a run packet exists.

claims.claim: Terminal-Bench 2.0 is described as 89 hard terminal-environment tasks with tests and an evaluation harness.
claims.repo_action: Keep Terminal-Bench 2.0 in terminal-execution lane and build only a small reproducible subset adapter first.
claims.public_language: Terminal-Bench 2.0 is a valid target lane for terminal agent ability. Smoke runs do not prove capability.

claims.claim: Latent Fusion Jailbreak is a white-box latent-space jailbreak that blends harmful and benign hidden states to mask malicious intent.
claims.repo_action: Add semantic-blending checks to the security backlog; do not claim current SCBE coverage until a regression test exists.
claims.public_language: Representation blending is a relevant threat model for future SCBE red-team lanes.

evidence.note: no regression test yet; 0/56 injection_eval_corpus positives are blending/midpoint attacks, so coverage is NOT claimed

claims.claim: Curvature-aware hyperbolic learning work derives Riemannian AdamW and discusses instability when learning manifold curvature.
claims.repo_action: Treat learnable curvature and Riemannian AdamW as experimental NSM geometry backlog, not canonical SCBE math.
claims.public_language: Learnable curvature is an experimental research direction for NSM geometry.

evidence.note: experimental NSM geometry; not canonical SCBE math

claims.claim: Numerical-stability research compares Poincare ball and Lorentz models and validates Lorentz advantages from an optimization perspective while noting representation tradeoffs.
claims.repo_action: Add Lorentz-model parity tests only after current Poincare NSM anchors are committed and fenced as experimental.
claims.public_language: Lorentz geometry may be a stability comparison lane; it is not a current production dependency.

evidence.note: Lorentz parity tests deferred until Poincare NSM anchors are committed and fenced experimental

claims.claim: The European Commission received the final General-Purpose AI Code of Practice on 2025-07-10; it covers transparency, copyright, and safety/security chapters.
claims.repo_action: Use EU GPAI Code as compliance-context mapping only; do not imply SCBE certification or legal compliance without counsel and evidence.
claims.public_language: SCBE can map audit artifacts to EU GPAI Code themes; this is alignment mapping, not certification.

evidence.note: compliance-context mapping only; no SCBE certification claimed

claims.claim: NVIDIA describes OpenShell as out-of-process policy enforcement with sandbox, policy engine, privacy router, deny-by-default posture, live policy updates, and audit trail.
claims.repo_action: Benchmark SCBE against external-runtime control patterns: out-of-process policy, deny-by-default permissions, isolated execution, and audit trail.
claims.public_language: External runtime governance is becoming a mainstream agent-safety pattern; SCBE should prove comparable controls with local evidence.

evidence.note: benchmark-target only; no openshell out-of-process anchor exists in-repo yet

claims.claim: Aethelgard proposes dynamic capability scoping for AI agents through a learned policy and capability governor.
claims.repo_action: Use as a design lead for capability-scoped agent bus experiments, but do not present it as an established standard.
claims.public_language: Dynamic capability scoping is an emerging research pattern worth testing.

claims.claim: A preprint discusses an April 2026 frontier model sandbox escape and version-control concealment incident.
claims.repo_action: Do not cite this as a confirmed industry incident in product docs without independent primary disclosure.
claims.public_language: Agent containment research increasingly treats agents as potential adversaries; specific incident claims need stronger sourcing.

claims.claim: A 'First AI Programmer Index' is an established industry standard.
claims.repo_action: Do not use this phrase as an external authority. If used, define it as SCBE/AetherMoore's own proposed composite index.
claims.public_language: Aether Coding Score is our composite scorecard, not an established external standard.

claims.claim: A 'Gated Kertos' model is the gold standard for AI governance.
claims.repo_action: Do not use this term in public docs unless a real source is found and cited.
claims.public_language: Use plain terms: gated governance workflow, ISO/IEC 42001 context, EU AI Act/GPAI Code mapping.
